classifications.meaning: Explicitly published content intended for unauthenticated or broad product visibility.
Low-risk operational metadata not intended for public display.
User, account, marketplace, creative, or commercial data requiring ownership and role controls.
requiredControls: encryption in transit and at rest

classifications.meaning: Credentials, private media, safety, moderation, security, raw inputs, or sensitive personal data.
API keys, signing secrets, authorization headers, encryption keys, private keys, or live session material.

purposes.meaning: Create and operate user accounts, profiles, settings, and sessions.
Authenticate users, authorize actions, prevent abuse, and respond to security incidents.
Publish, propose, deliver, review, dispute, and settle internal task rewards.
Publish and interact with community, profile, portfolio, and library content.
Create, persist, review, recover, and deliver Image, Chat, Video, and Music outputs.
Upload, scan, quarantine, store, preview, and privately download media.
Apply content policy, investigate reports, review outputs, and handle appeals.
Operate non-withdrawable points, creative credits, quotas, escrow, and compensation.
Provide user support, observability, incident response, and audited Admin operations.
Meet validated legal holds, rights requests, contractual duties, and evidence obligations.

exampleFields: safe safety disposition and reason codes

requiredControls: no secret or raw Provider payload
no child-process or log inheritance beyond need
durable replay claim before side effects
no private URL or raw prompt

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: Up to 30 days in abuse-monitoring logs; no application-state retention for image generation and edits.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: supported deployment and user-access footprint only
creativeProviders.deletionEvidence: contract configuration and Provider support evidence

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: API prediction inputs, outputs, files, values, and logs are removed after one hour by default.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: customer and end-user geography must be approved
creativeProviders.deletionEvidence: prediction deletion behavior and contract evidence

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: Up to 30 days in abuse-monitoring logs with store=false and background mode disabled.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: supported deployment and user-access footprint only
creativeProviders.deletionEvidence: store=false/ZDR configuration and Provider support evidence

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: API inputs and outputs are deleted within 30 days, with documented policy, legal, safety, and feedback exceptions.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: supported-country access and US storage must be approved

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: Request/response logging is off by default; abuse logging may apply and in-memory caching can last up to 24 hours.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: approved us-central1 processing and cross-border review

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: Ephemeral uploads expire after 24 hours and output URLs within 24-48 hours; broader retention requires contract confirmation.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: access geography, data transfers, and no-training enterprise terms required

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: US hosting by default; Music content deletion period requires Enterprise contract confirmation.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: eligible Enterprise Music region and ZRM support must be contracted
creativeProviders.deletionEvidence: Enterprise Music order and deletion receipt

creativeProviders.defaultRetention: General abuse-monitoring and cache rules apply; Preview-specific retention requires written confirmation.
creativeProviders.regionCondition: global Preview processing requires legal and contract approval

exportRules: exclude credentials and internal detection logic

availableCapabilities: User has an explicit deleted status
refresh sessions can be revoked and have expiry timestamps
media scan history supports archive-before-prune with 180-day and per-asset defaults
creative generation records store prompt hash and bounded preview instead of a raw prompt field
Provider adapter contracts reject secret-like metadata keys
Provider HTTP client uses a fixed endpoint, minimum payload, deployment-only secret injection, and default-disabled network gate
Provider callback API uses exact-body HMAC, nonce/job binding, strict projection, zero-retention raw payload handling, and atomic replay claims
Provider polling uses a fixed read-only status endpoint, strict response projection, safe worker summaries, durable due-time and attempt-budget retry state, and idempotent timeout recovery
Provider failures use a shared safe taxonomy, bounded Retry-After, deterministic backoff, hash-only failure dedupe, and CAS retry state

Provider output ingestion uses a durable source key, claim lease, allowlisted injected fetch, bounded byte reads, magic-byte MIME validation, SHA-256, deterministic storage, and scan-gated media assets
Provider dispatch uses versioned fail-closed controls, expiring hashed cap evidence, explicit circuit recovery, one-claim probes, two-person review, and low-cardinality operations evidence

Chat has encrypted owner-scoped conversations and messages, idempotent Mock SSE turns, strict owner attachment checks, explicitly selected read-time-authorized product context, identity-free input/output safety evidence, a 512-character unclassified output buffer, explicit stop/disconnect closeout, retention sweeps, restore-deletion tombstones, and a default-disabled staging Provider boundary whose attachment bytes and Provider payloads remain in request memory only
Admin creative serializers redact unsafe identifiers, URLs, and error previews
archive and object writers support mock/S3-compatible boundaries
knownGaps: no account export API or package builder
no field-level retention sweeper outside media scan history
no backup expiry and restore deletion rehearsal evidence
no global structured log allowlist enforcement
current foreign-key behavior can cascade or restrict deletion without the frozen anonymization plan
no legal-hold registry or expiry workflow
no account-level Chat export/deletion orchestration beyond owner conversation deletion and restore tombstones

implementationHandoff.scope: Provider secrets and minimum-payload HTTP client boundary.
Provider callback authentication, strict payload minimization, job binding, replay idempotency, and safe audit boundary.
Provider status polling projection, retry scheduling, timeout recovery, worker isolation, and safe audit boundary.
Idempotent generation cancel/retry mutations, child-attempt linkage, reviewed manual replay, and raw-prompt/provider-payload exclusion.
Source-keyed Provider output ingestion, SSRF and byte boundaries, MIME/checksum validation, deterministic storage, scanner gating, and raw-URL exclusion.
Provider-independent pricing snapshots, six-decimal cost normalization, durable atomic budget reservation, idempotent settlement/release/reconciliation, and low-cardinality operations evidence.
Versioned Provider kill switches, expiring Provider-side cap evidence, explicit circuit breaking and one-claim probes, two-person recovery review, and safe operations evidence.
Freeze the Chat persistence, retention, export, deletion, backup, and Provider-state contract.
Implement encrypted owner-scoped conversations and messages, inactivity expiry, export/delete hooks, and restore-deletion replay.
Authorize Chat attachment metadata and selected product context, enforce input and buffered streaming safety, and persist identity-free review evidence without attachment bytes or raw context copies.

Implement default-disabled Chat Provider, classifier, exact-size attachment-byte, Provider-control, and cost-ledger boundaries while retaining no raw Provider payloads, attachment bytes, or resolved context bodies.
OAuth scopes, identifiers, unlink, region, and session evidence.
PostgreSQL migration, encrypted backup, expiry, restore, and deletion rehearsal.
Object storage/CDN private access and lifecycle deletion.
Scanner request/callback minimization, isolation, retention, and deletion.
Observability and notification processor retention and redaction.
Managed secrets, runtime injection, rotation, and revocation.
Image safety-event data minimization and deletion propagation.
Chat message/context retention and streaming safety evidence.
Video input/output lifecycle and provenance retention.
Music input/output, license, and takedown retention.
Review, appeal, legal hold, and safety audit implementation.
Account export, deletion, anonymization, and per-store evidence orchestration.
Admin least privilege, elevated-read audit, and high-risk controls.
Privacy, secret, supply-chain, and deletion rehearsal release review.
Privacy notice, AUP, user rights, processor disclosure, and support entry points.
